compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — FAIL

Verified against four Acceptance Criteria. Two are NOT MET.

---

### AC1 — Editor renders `batch` step's criteria list + supports ADD/REMOVE/EDIT + renders batch params and `if:` overlay predicate

**NOT MET.**

The Python-side serializer (`bpmn.py`) does emit `batch` steps correctly: a `bpmn:serviceTask` + `<rebar:Batch finder=…>` marker, with the full `batch` dict (criteria list, usd_budget, model_ladder) riding in `<rebar:Config>` (lines 280–292). Round-trip reconstruction also works (lines 654–661). However, the **front-end properties panel** (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx`) has **no batch-specific UI at all**:

- `rebarKind()` (lines 40–57) returns `"agent"` for **all** `bpmn:ServiceTask` elements — `batch` and `agent` steps are indistinguishable in the UI.
- `STRUCTURED_KINDS` (line 59) is `["scripted", "agent", "loop", "map"]` — `"batch"` is absent.
- `structuredEntries()` (lines 475–544) has no `batch` case — no criteria list, no add/remove/edit UX, no usd_budget / model_ladder / finder fields.
- No dedicated `if:` predicate field exists anywhere in the panel for prompt steps.

A `batch` step selected in the editor shows the `agent` panel (Prompt id, mode, model, etc.) — NOT the criteria list. The core AC requirement — that the editor **renders and edits the criteria list** — is unimplemented in the front end.

---

### AC2 — Round-trip preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text; add/remove-criterion round-trips losslessly

**MET.** The Python-side round-trip is fully implemented and verified by `test_editor_batch.py` (tests at lines 54–93): `test_batch_round_trip_is_lossless`, `test_add_criterion_round_trips`, `test_remove_criterion_round_trips`, `test_edit_criterion_prompt_and_when_round_trips`, and `test_reconstructed_batch_is_schema_valid_and_lint_clean` all cover the lossless IR→BPMN→IR data round-trip with schema validation. The `if:` overlay also round-trips at line 104–121.

---

### AC3 — An editor test covers rendering + add/remove/edit of a criterion in the batch criteria list

**NOT MET.**

`tests/unit/workflow/test_editor_batch.py` tests the **data-level** Python round-trip for add/remove/edit of criteria. It does not test:
- **Rendering** in the editor panel (no browser/E2E assertions that the criteria list is visible or structured fields are presented).
- **UI-level ADD/REMOVE/EDIT** through the properties panel (no browser script driving the batch step panel).

No browser E2E script (in `tests/e2e/js/`) covers batch criteria. The browser tests (`test_editor_browser.py`) have zero coverage of batch/criteria. The test file covers the data contract but not the "rendering" requirement the AC states.

---

### AC4 — make lint + relevant editor/workflow tests pass

**Cannot verify** from static file inspection (requires running the suite). The tests that exist do cover the Python round-trip. However, since the front-end batch UI is absent (AC1 unmet), any browser E2E test for batch rendering would be absent too, so this criterion's completeness is tied to AC1/AC3.

---

**Summary:** The Python-side serializer correctly handles `batch` steps and the round-trip is lossless and schema-valid. But the front-end properties panel (`rebarProvider.jsx`) has no `batch`-specific rendering — it treats batch steps as agent steps, shows no criteria list, and offers no ADD/REMOVE/EDIT UX for criteria. No browser-level test covers rendering + editing of the criteria list. AC1 and AC3 are not met.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Completion verifier (correctly) blocked the first close: A4's ACs require the bpmn-js FRONT-END to render/edit the batch criteria + the if: overlay, but the initial commit only did the Python-side IR<->BPMN round-trip. Remediated by building the real front-end: rebarProvider.jsx now (1) distinguishes a batch ServiceTask from an agent one via cfg.batch, (2) renders finder/usd_budget/model_ladder + a 'Batch criteria' ListGroup with add/remove/edit of each criterion (prompt id + optional when overlay), (3) adds an if: overlay-predicate field to scripted/agent/batch steps, and (4) adds a ServiceTask kind toggle (agent<->batch) so a batch is authorable from a freshly-drawn node. A real-browser Playwright E2E (browser_batch.mjs + test_editor_batch_criteria_render_add_remove_edit) drives a v3 fixture and asserts render+add+remove+edit+conversion+persist-to-IR; verified passing in headless Chromium. The agent->batch toggle resolves a code-review MINOR (couldn't create a batch step from scratch). Commits 974dfce2 (Python round-trip) + 26454cd9 (front-end).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

Verified all four Acceptance Criteria against the current implementation (post-remediation commit 26454cd9).

---

### AC1 — Editor renders `batch` step's criteria list + supports ADD/REMOVE/EDIT + renders batch params and `if:` overlay predicate

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx` now has full batch-step front-end support:

- **`isBatch()`** (lines 41–44): distinguishes a batch `ServiceTask` from an agent one via `cfg.batch` being a non-null object.
- **`rebarKind()`** (line 53): returns `"batch"` for batch ServiceTasks (no longer conflates them with `"agent"`).
- **`STRUCTURED_KINDS`** (line 67): now includes `"batch"`.
- **Batch param fields** (lines 523–626): `BatchFinderEntry` (finder/prompt), `BatchNumberEntry` (usd_budget), `BatchLadderEntry` (model_ladder, comma-separated).
- **`CriterionFieldEntry`** (lines 630–668): reads/writes a single `prompt`/`when` slice of `cfg.batch.criteria[index]` via `mutateConfig`.
- **`batchCriteriaGroup()`** (lines 674–736): a `ListGroup` rendering one collapsible item per criterion, with add (`cr.push({prompt: ""})`) and remove (`cr.splice(i, 1)`) callbacks wired.
- **`structuredEntries()` batch case** (lines 796–821): pushes ServiceKindEntry, BatchFinderEntry, BatchNumberEntry, BatchLadderEntry, and the `if:` overlay entry.
- **`if:` overlay field** (lines 758–769): `ifEntry` using `ConfigTextEntry` with `ckey="if"` is added to scripted, agent, and batch entries.
- **`ServiceKindEntry`** (lines 487–514): a closed select that converts an agent step into a batch step (seeds `cfg.batch`) or back (drops it), enabling batch authoring from a freshly-drawn node.
- **`getGroups()`** (lines 924–929): pushes the `batchCriteriaGroup` as a second panel group whenever `rebarKind(bo) === "batch"`.

---

### AC2 — Round-trip preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text; add/remove-criterion round-trips losslessly

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/workflow/bpmn.py` (lines 280–292) serializes a `batch` step as `bpmn:serviceTask` with `<rebar:Config>` carrying the full `batch` dict (criteria list, usd_budget, model_ladder). `tests/unit/workflow/test_editor_batch.py` covers `test_batch_round_trip_is_lossless`, `test_add_criterion_round_trips`, `test_remove_criterion_round_trips`, `test_edit_criterion_prompt_and_when_round_trips`, `test_reconstructed_batch_is_schema_valid_and_lint_clean`, and `test_prompt_step_if_overlay_round_trips` (lines 54–121).

---

### AC3 — An editor test covers rendering + add/remove/edit of a criterion in the batch criteria list

**MET.**

- `tests/e2e/js/browser_batch.mjs` (lines 1–208): a real Playwright browser probe that (1) RENDERS the batch step's finder/budget/ladder fields and criteria list (including a `when` overlay), (2) EDITs criterion-0's prompt into `rebar:Config`, (3) ADDs a criterion and checks count, (4) REMOVEs a criterion and checks count, (5) verifies the `if:` overlay field on a prompt step and the agent↔batch kind toggle, and (6) saves to IR.
- `tests/e2e/test_editor_browser.py` (lines 97–160): `test_editor_batch_criteria_render_add_remove_edit` drives that script and asserts all of rendering/add/remove/edit/overlay/save at the browser level.
- `tests/e2e/conftest.py` (lines 139–157): `editor_server_batch` fixture serves the `batch-demo.yaml` fixture.
- `tests/e2e/fixtures/batch-demo.yaml`: minimal v3 batch fixture with a batch step (2 criteria, one `when`-gated), plus a prompt step carrying an `if:` overlay.

---

### AC4 — make lint + relevant editor/workflow tests pass

**No red flags found.** All test infrastructure (Python unit tests, browser E2E, fixtures, conftest wiring) is present and properly connected. No `skip`/`xfail`/`TODO` markers were found in the batch-specific code or test files. Cannot statically assert a clean run, but the implementation and test suite are complete.

---

**Overall: PASS.** The previous failure (front-end had no batch UI) has been fully remediated. The front-end properties panel now renders and edits batch steps with criteria add/remove/edit, batch params, the `if:` overlay predicate, and an agent↔batch kind toggle, all verified by a real-browser Playwright E2E test.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The core driver of the visual-editing goal is letting developers ADD / REMOVE / EDIT individual
CRITERIA. With the A2 shape, the batch step's `criteria` list is a static authored list of
prompt-library-backed entries — discrete nodes the editor can represent and modify. Proving the
add/remove-criterion round-trip end-to-end is the editability deliverable AND part of A's de-risking
of B (the criteria-library <-> prompt-library <-> editor wiring).

## What

Extend the workflow visual editor (docs/workflow-editor.md) to render and edit a `batch` step's
`criteria` list — each entry a prompt-library-backed criterion with its optional `when` overlay —
and support ADD / REMOVE / EDIT of a criterion. Also render/edit the `batch` params
(prompt/finder, usd_budget, model_ladder) and a prompt step's `if:` overlay predicate (the existing
field; confirm + extend only as needed). Preserve the engine invariants: clean git diffs +
schema-validated text round-trip (the e6d9 editor invariant). The demo workflow is the refinement sample.

## Scope

- The workflow editor surface (bpmn-js / editor assets + the Python render/round-trip; see
  docs/workflow-editor.md + tests/e2e/test_editor_* / tests/unit/workflow/test_bpmn.py).
- Render + edit the `batch` step (criteria list + params) and the `if:` overlay predicate;
  ADD/REMOVE/EDIT a prompt-backed criterion in the criteria list.
- Round-trip keeps clean git diffs + schema validation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The editor renders a `batch` step's `criteria` list (each a prompt-library-backed entry + its
      `when`) and supports ADD / REMOVE / EDIT of a criterion; it also renders the batch params and a
      prompt step's `if:` overlay predicate.
- [ ] Editor round-trip preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text (the e6d9 invariant) — an
      add/remove-criterion round-trips losslessly.
- [ ] An editor test covers rendering + add/remove/edit of a criterion in the batch criteria list.
- [ ] make lint + the relevant editor/workflow tests pass.
compiled_state.title: Visual editing for the new constructs (overlay predicate + batch step) in the workflow editor
